claims.protected_property: Byte-identical registered W8A8 execution across independent CPU, AMD, and NVIDIA implementations.
claims.adversary: Rounding, saturation, tokenizer, accumulation-order, compiler, driver, and firmware divergence.
claims.metric: Mismatch count over at least 10^9 operator instances and full tokenizer/KV/logit/greedy-decode paths.
claims.pass_threshold: Zero mismatch across two independently authored CPU implementations and registered AMD and NVIDIA kernels.
claims.kill_threshold: Any unexplained mismatch or common implementation lineage represented as independent.
claims.rollback: Keep public WWM inference disabled and retain the mini profile as test-only.
owner_blockers: Signed WWM deterministic-profile owner unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent CPU implementation and NVIDIA hardware evidence are not locally available.

claims.mechanism_id: Public inference latency throughput and failure
claims.protected_property: Interactive three-operator inference without degrading base consensus.
claims.adversary: Concurrency, slow workers, crashes, packet loss, model reload, and chain saturation.
claims.metric: Committed-token p50/p95/p99, completion, goodput, memory, evidence bytes, refunds, and base finality impact.
claims.pass_threshold: p95 below 2 seconds, p99 below 5 seconds, at least 99.9% completion, and base p95 degradation below 5%.
claims.kill_threshold: Any false ASSURED result, unbounded queue, or base degradation at or above 5% under registered load.
claims.rollback: Disable public WWM scheduling and refund or expire open jobs.
owner_blockers: Signed public-load profile unresolved.
external_blockers: Three independent live control clusters and public load are required.

claims.protected_property: Content-addressed model availability under independent churn without consensus weight storage.
claims.adversary: Corrupt, replayed, unavailable, and common-control shards plus regional churn.
claims.metric: Thirty-day retrieval, reconstruction latency, repair bandwidth, false availability, and concentration.
claims.pass_threshold: At least 99.9% required retrieval, all corrupt/replayed shards rejected, and every permitted loss reconstructed.
claims.kill_threshold: Common-control loss falsely satisfies policy or an unavailable model remains schedulable.
claims.rollback: Freeze new jobs for the affected capsule and repair from the last available manifest.
owner_blockers: Signed custody policy unresolved.
external_blockers: Five custodians in three regions and thirty real days are required.

claims.protected_property: Objective fault isolation without false slashing or unrelated-job interruption.
claims.adversary: Wrong operators, equivocation, unavailable evidence, frivolous challenges, and deadline griefing.
claims.metric: Detection, false slash, rounds, transactions, bytes, terminal time, payoff, and replay correctness.
claims.pass_threshold: All injected objective faults detected, zero false slash over at least 10^6 honest chunks, and T=32 terminal below six hours.
claims.kill_threshold: Any honest execution can be slashed or evidence unavailability advances assurance.
claims.rollback: Disable slash disposition, retain refunds and bounded exclusion, and keep base consensus unaffected.
owner_blockers: Signed WWM bond and dispute policy unresolved.
external_blockers: Public testnet committee and funded independent challengers are required.

claims.protected_property: Prompt-key release only to fresh admitted CPU/GPU confidential workloads with no prohibited plaintext persistence.
claims.adversary: Stale or revoked firmware, wrong workload, quote replay, rollback, host logging, crash, DMA, and verifier outage.
claims.metric: Accept/reject matrix, canary forensic findings, quote latency, TTFT, revocation propagation, and fail-closed recovery.
claims.pass_threshold: All invalid quote cases reject before key release, no canary reaches prohibited surfaces, and P1 p95 TTFT stays below five seconds.
claims.kill_threshold: Plaintext escapes the declared boundary or any path downgrades to nonconfidential execution.
claims.rollback: Disable P1 key release, revoke affected measurements, and refund private jobs.
owner_blockers: Supported vendor, firmware, and revocation policy unresolved.
external_blockers: Supported confidential CPU/GPU platforms and independent forensic review are required.

claims.protected_property: Complete registered private transformer relation at a useful measured envelope.
claims.adversary: Incomplete nonlinear, KV, logit, decoding coverage; collusion; active faults; and one-layer extrapolation.
claims.metric: Seconds per token, proof/verifier time, proof size, client traffic, memory, abort, leakage, and relation coverage.
claims.pass_threshold: P3 at most five seconds and 25 MB per token, or P2 at most 30 seconds per 32-token proof chunk, 200 ms verification, and 1 MB proof with full coverage.
claims.kill_threshold: Incomplete relation marketed as complete, hidden trust, one-layer extrapolation, or cost above ten times P1 without distinct required security benefit.
claims.rollback: Keep the failing suite disabled without changing P0, P1, or any sibling suite.
owner_blockers: Private-backend product envelope unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent cryptanalysis and full-model backend hardware are required.

claims.protected_property: No material prompt recovery, membership inference, or stable cross-job linking from private public metadata.
claims.adversary: Dictionary, membership, semantic reconstruction, length, timing, and chosen-prompt attacks.
claims.metric: Canary recovery, membership AUC, semantic recovery, length classification, and linkability.
claims.pass_threshold: Canary recovery within control plus one point, membership AUC at most 0.55, and no stable cross-job identifier.
claims.kill_threshold: A deterministic secret-derived public commitment supports dictionary recovery or stable linking.
claims.rollback: Withdraw the leaking commitment/receipt profile and rotate its domain and keys.
owner_blockers: Published leakage budget unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent privacy attack review is required.

claims.protected_property: Single noncolluding route position cannot bind client IP to destination under the declared observer model.
claims.adversary: Malicious ingress or egress, loss, churn, DNS and WebRTC leaks, and direct-fallback traps.
claims.metric: Linkability AUC, route overhead, completion, address leaks, and fallback behavior over 100,000 sessions.
claims.pass_threshold: Linkability within baseline plus five points, p95 overhead below 500 ms, at least 99.9% completion, and zero direct fallback.
claims.kill_threshold: Common-control ingress/egress is required or any private failure becomes a direct request.
claims.rollback: Disable fast-private selection and retain explicit direct mode only when the user selects it.
owner_blockers: Route control-cluster policy unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent multi-operator route deployment is required.

claims.protected_property: Padded mixing materially reduces ingress-egress correlation under a preregistered global passive observer.
claims.adversary: Global capture, active delay, drop, watermark, small anonymity set, and operator concentration.
claims.metric: Linking AUC, anonymity entropy, 256 KiB latency, bandwidth amplification, delivery, and concentration.
claims.pass_threshold: Linking AUC at most 0.55, p95 fetch below five seconds, amplification at most five times, and at least 99% delivery.
claims.kill_threshold: Cover traffic does not materially improve on fast mode or topology invalidates the observer model.
claims.rollback: Keep deep routing disabled and do not strengthen fast-route claims.
owner_blockers: Deep-mix topology and cover budget unresolved.
external_blockers: Live multi-region operators and independent traffic analysis are required.

claims.protected_property: Publisher/content origins and reproducible signed builds prevent shared-gateway privilege and unauthorized updates.
claims.adversary: Malicious gateways, origin confusion, service workers, revoked signers, downgrade, split-view logs, and dependency substitution.
claims.metric: Cross-origin access, permission bleed, bad-update acceptance, reproducibility hash equality, and rollback.
claims.pass_threshold: Zero cross-origin privilege, every invalid update rejected, two independent matching builders, and rollback on every platform.
claims.kill_threshold: A shared path merges origins or one online signer can silently replace the browser.
claims.rollback: Revoke the build/signer and restore the last valid reproducible artifact.
owner_blockers: Browser signer and engine-maintenance policy unresolved.
external_blockers: Two independent builders and browser security review are required.

claims.protected_property: Staged intake prevents high-risk poison from active retrieval without suppressing legitimate corrections.
claims.adversary: Twenty-percent Sybil contributors, copied sources, plausible falsehoods, stale facts, invalid licenses, malware, and PII.
claims.metric: Poison quarantine, false decisions, top-k contamination, correction latency, source clusters, and reviewer concentration.
claims.pass_threshold: At least 95% high-risk poison quarantined, under 5% false quarantine, and zero unauthorized or malicious training artifacts.
claims.kill_threshold: Stake or duplicate keys alone manufacture independent corroboration.
claims.rollback: Quarantine the affected snapshot, restore its parent, and publish correction/revocation edges.
owner_blockers: Knowledge policy and domain-review owners unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent domain reviewers and attack corpus are required.

claims.protected_property: Confidential cohort updates preserve individual privacy while bounding aggregate poisoning under explicit limits.
claims.adversary: One-third dropout, twenty-percent Byzantine workers, backdoor, replacement, malformed range, and attribution ambiguity.
claims.metric: Leakage, convergence, clean/backdoor quality, aggregate norm, abort, attribution, and communication.
claims.pass_threshold: No individual update revealed, at most two-point clean degradation, backdoor below ceiling, and completion at dropout bound.
claims.kill_threshold: A hidden update causes a critical undetected backdoor or the system claims unavailable individual accountability.
claims.rollback: Keep the confidential cohort lane shadow-only and reject its candidate snapshot.
owner_blockers: Confidential cohort adversary and quality ceilings unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent federated-learning red team is required.

claims.protected_property: Independent gates catch critical regressions before full activation and restore an immutable parent.
claims.adversary: Capability regression, backdoor, memorization, citation failure, latency spike, privacy violation, and evaluator capture.
claims.metric: Detection stage, false promotion/rejection, exposed traffic, rollback latency, consistency, and parent availability.
claims.pass_threshold: All critical seeds blocked, at least 95% other seeded regressions detected, at most 5% critical exposure, and parent rollback succeeds.
claims.kill_threshold: A sponsor suppresses a failing committed report or activation mutates the parent.
claims.rollback: Move the serving alias to the registered parent and freeze the candidate.
owner_blockers: Evaluator and activation threshold owners unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent evaluator families and hidden suites are required.

claims.protected_property: Application escrow makes provable cheating unprofitable without centralizing selection.
claims.adversary: Fake demand, circular funding, underpricing, withholding, challenger griefing, cloud Sybils, and sponsor abuse.
claims.metric: Expected returns, false slash/refund, completion, price dispersion, HHI, maximum cluster share, and sponsor cost.
claims.pass_threshold: No positive modeled return for provable cheating, positive bounded honest return, and no cluster above 25% pilot selection/custody.
claims.kill_threshold: Cheapest-wrong dominates or key diversity hides common control.
claims.rollback: Retain application-only fees, disable concentration-violating selection, and keep all consensus economics zero.
owner_blockers: WWM fee, bond, sponsor, and concentration policy unresolved.
external_blockers: Real independent demand and operator data are required.

claims.protected_property: Base consensus remains safe and live during complete AI shutdown while service state recovers explicitly.
claims.adversary: Rolling upgrade, region loss, model reload, index rebuild, custodian churn, and seven-day AI blackout.
claims.metric: Query/base uptime, recovery, finality, stale state, false assurance, and private-log findings.
claims.pass_threshold: At least 99.9% service completion during service window, zero base safety failure, bounded base SLO, and explicit refund/expiry.
claims.kill_threshold: Base consensus depends on AI liveness or blackout needs unsafe manual state edits.
claims.rollback: Disable WWM services while keeping base nodes and transfers live.
owner_blockers: Public operation profile and incident owners unresolved.
external_blockers: Thirty-day service, seven real blackout days, and public operators are required.

claims.protected_property: Users can query, choose privacy accurately, inspect trust, and contribute without protocol expertise.
claims.adversary: Claim confusion, inaccessible UI, low bandwidth, small screens, keyboard-only use, and misleading privacy language.
claims.metric: Task completion/time, privacy comprehension, trust confusion, rights accuracy, recovery, and WCAG findings.
claims.pass_threshold: At least 80% unassisted completion, median first query below 30 seconds, at least 80% correct trust answers, and no critical WCAG 2.2 AA blockers.
claims.kill_threshold: Users consistently mistake transport encryption for executor privacy or execution assurance for truth.
claims.rollback: Withdraw misleading UI, retain explicit advanced diagnostics, and redesign before rollout.
owner_blockers: User-research cohort and localization owners unresolved.
external_blockers: Representative independent participants and accessibility audit are required.

claims.protected_property: Rights and revocation control future canonical use without false erasure claims.
claims.adversary: Visibility/training conflation, post-snapshot revocation, derived artifacts, contributor key loss, and historical rewrite.
claims.metric: Unauthorized inclusion, exclusion latency, lineage, historical receipt resolution, and UI comprehension.
claims.pass_threshold: Every ineligible/revoked item is excluded from the next snapshot/dataset and every prior use remains traceable.
claims.kill_threshold: Public visibility becomes training consent or revocation rewrites historical roots.
claims.rollback: Restore the parent snapshot and block the rights-invalid candidate.
owner_blockers: Rights-policy owner and counsel review unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent rights/legal review is required before public production use.

claims.protected_property: Subthreshold compromised roles cannot activate bytes; emergency rollback remains narrow and expiring.
claims.adversary: Stolen proposer, trainer, evaluator, activator, route, build, or emergency keys plus censorship and split view.
claims.metric: Unauthorized acceptance, detection, scope, old-revision pinning, emergency expiry, and fork/exit.
claims.pass_threshold: Every subthreshold coalition rejects, time lock holds, emergency action only pauses/reverts and expires, and parent remains queryable.
claims.kill_threshold: One role or model-controlled key activates a revision or seizes user keys.
claims.rollback: Revoke compromised keys, revert the alias, and retain immutable history and user pinning.
owner_blockers: WWM governance and emergency key sets unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent ceremony participants and governance red team are required.

claims.mechanism_id: Private cache memory log and telemetry leakage
claims.protected_property: Private plaintext does not persist or cross tenant/job boundaries.
claims.adversary: Cancel, timeout, crash, reboot, OOM, GPU reset, snapshot, telemetry outage, and concurrent tenant forensics.
claims.metric: Canary occurrences in RAM, VRAM, disk, swap, logs, crash, metrics, cross-job cache, and wipe latency.
claims.pass_threshold: No canary in prohibited persistence, no cross-user cache reuse, crash dumps disabled or user-encrypted, and wipe within bound.
claims.kill_threshold: Private prompts enter shared cache or operational telemetry.
claims.rollback: Disable the affected private runtime and destroy its cache and telemetry material.
owner_blockers: Private wipe and forensic budget unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent confidential-computing forensic review is required.

claims.protected_property: Every chain, model, profile, route, build, quote, and revocation mismatch fails closed.
claims.adversary: Foreign chain/genesis, stale model/profile, wrong privacy tuple, unavailable route, revoked build, and gateway substitution.
claims.metric: False accept/reject, direct fallback, displayed mode, and settlement class over critical tuple combinations.
claims.pass_threshold: Every invalid combination rejects, zero silent downgrade, exact settlement class, and valid independent-client interoperability.
claims.kill_threshold: A private request executes publicly or a foreign chain/model receipt displays as valid.
claims.rollback: Fail the job/build/route, preserve the selected privacy boundary, and refund where applicable.
owner_blockers: Cross-client test matrix owner unresolved.
external_blockers: Second independent WWM client is required for full evidence.

claims.protected_property: Canonical public retrieval reproduces and private retrieval stays within its leakage budget.
claims.adversary: Hardware nondeterminism, adversarial ties, query/access-pattern attacks, and public-index fallback.
claims.metric: Top-k/root equality, citation spans, query/access leakage, latency, and rebuild time.
claims.pass_threshold: Independent canonical builders produce identical roots/top-k and private retrieval meets E-WWM-07 without public calls.
claims.kill_threshold: A nondeterministic index supports a slashable claim or private queries enter public logs.
claims.rollback: Mark the index advisory or restore the parent snapshot; fail private retrieval closed.
owner_blockers: Canonical retrieval profile owner unresolved.
external_blockers: Second independent index implementation is required.

claims.protected_property: Training policy detects unacceptable memorization and excludes unauthorized private contributions.
claims.adversary: Canary extraction, membership inference, PII-like strings, sealed examples, adapter merge, and rights substitution.
claims.metric: Exact extraction, exposure, membership AUC, utility impact, unauthorized inclusion, and rights lineage.
claims.pass_threshold: No unauthorized/sealed item enters the dataset, extraction/membership stay below registered ceilings, and every item has rights lineage.
claims.kill_threshold: Private data is included by default or activation relies on an unmeasured forgetting claim.
claims.rollback: Reject the dataset and candidate, revoke future snapshot use, and preserve the audit record.
owner_blockers: Memorization ceilings and privacy evaluator unresolved.
external_blockers: Independent extraction and privacy evaluation are required.
